ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: Yirbəə wakatɩ kaakʊ zeezi bɩ, Yeezuu zee gʊta da, a ʊ: «Eloyii, Eloyii, lama sabaktaani?» A lɛ taa k'a hɩ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ Woso, mɔɔ Woso, bɔ m, ɩ ɩ bɔ mɔɔ ʊ?»
translated: And about the ninth hour, Jesus cried out with a loud voice, saying: “Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?” that is, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: Naa minto bɩ, a b'a zu a ʊ y, bala, awɔɔ n dɔmɩm k'awɔɔ Zuuba n nɩ zɛŋ ʊ bɩ dɔ y.
translated: When I came, you were not there, but I met your mother and she told me you had gone to Zuba

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Gʊɔɔ bʊr nyɩnɔ poorero m Yeezuu zi, k'an a wɔ ka ŋ mim la, an yaa da ŋ tɔ ma, bɩ karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n yɛ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ nɩ y, ŋn'a bam kan ŋ kɩ.
translated: When Jesus had finished speaking, a centurion came to him, begging him and saying, "My servant is lying at home paralyzed and in terrible pain."

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: Kʊsɩ ɩ kan awɔɔ kɩ, awɔɔ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ zambʊzannɔ kɩ, awɔɔ ɩ tam kʊrɔ ʊ tara burə ʊ kan kɔɔlʊʊ nɔ ʊ kɩ an n'a kam gʊaa deem taan… ma k'an zu awɔɔ lɛrɔ wɔɔ ʊ, bɩ k'awɔɔ ku b'awɔɔ ka an yɩ sɛhar gʊaa gallɛm hɩɩya an lɛ da awɔɔ m.
translated: When I was still a child, my father used to tell me that when he was a young man, he used to go to the farm with his father to clear the land and plant crops, and after harvesting, they would share the crops among the family members, and the remaining would be stored in a barn for future use...

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Bɩ, kʊ zibəənyɩ bʊnyaa m, a y'a hɩrɛ a heer ʊ, a ʊ, mɔɔ Zuuba maasɩrɛ ba a bɩr zɛrɛ y.
translated: They, all the people of the village, when they see him, they run and go to tell the chief that a stranger has come.
